contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Pointed Truncations.
From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Pointed Truncations.
Require Import HSpace Suspension ExactSequence HomotopyGroup.
(* Group not imported, as it confuses Rocq regarding [*.] notation. *)
Require Import WildCat.Core WildCat.Universe WildCat.Equiv Modalities.ReflectiveSubuniverse Modalities.Descent.

(** * The Hopf construction *)

(** We define the Hopf construction associated to a left-invertible H-space, and use it to prove that H-spaces satisfy a strengthened version of Freudenthal's theorem (see [freudenthal_hspace] below). *)

(** The Hopf construction associated to a left-invertible H-space (Definition 8.5.6 in the HoTT book). *)

goals.conclusion: Susp_rec X X (fun x : X => path_universe (sg_op x)) pt

contents:
(** ** Total space of the Hopf construction *)

(** The total space of the Hopf construction on [Susp X] is the join of [X] with itself. Note that we need both left and right multiplication to be equivalences. This is true when [X] is a 0-connected H-space for example. (This is lemma 8.5.7 in the HoTT book). *)

hypotheses.type: forall a : X, IsEquiv (fun y : X => y * a)
forall a : X, IsEquiv (fun y : X => y * a)
forall a : X, IsEquiv (fun y : X => y * a)
forall a : X, IsEquiv (fun y : X => y * a)
forall a : X, IsEquiv (fun y : X => y * a)

refine (_ oE equiv_pod_flatten (f := const_tt X) (g := const_tt X)
    (@Build_poDescent _ _ _ (const_tt X) (const_tt X)
    (Unit_ind (pointed_type X)) (Unit_ind (pointed_type X))
    (fun x => Build_Equiv _ _ (x *.) (H1 x)))).

goals.conclusion:
Pushout (functor_sigma (const_tt X) (fun a : X => idmap))
  (functor_sigma (const_tt X)
     (fun a : X =>
      pod_e
        {|
          pod_faml := Unit_ind X;
          pod_famr := Unit_ind X;
          pod_e :=
            fun x : X => {| equiv_fun := sg_op x; equiv_isequiv := H1 x |}
        |} a)) <~>
pjoin X X

?eB o functor_sigma (const_tt X) (fun a : X => idmap) ==
fst
o (equiv_sigma_prod0 X X
   oE equiv_functor_sigma_id
        (fun x : X =>

         fun x : X => {| equiv_fun := sg_op x; equiv_isequiv := H1 x |}
     |} (const_tt X H3)} <~>
   X * X)

goals.conclusion:
(equiv_pushout
   (equiv_sigma_prod0 X X
    oE equiv_functor_sigma_id
         (fun x : X =>
          {| equiv_fun := fun y : X => y * x; equiv_isequiv := H2 x |})
    oE equiv_sigma_symm0 X X
    :
    {H3 : X &
    pod_faml
      {|
        pod_faml := Unit_ind X;
        pod_famr := Unit_ind X;
        pod_e :=
          fun x : X => {| equiv_fun := sg_op x; equiv_isequiv := H1 x |}
      |} (const_tt X H3)} <~>
    X * X)
   (equiv_contr_sigma (Unit_ind X)) (equiv_contr_sigma (Unit_ind X))
   (fun x0 : {_ : X & X} => 1%path) (fun x0 : {_ : X & X} => 1%path)
 oE equiv_pod_flatten
      {|
        pod_faml := Unit_ind X;
        pod_famr := Unit_ind X;
        pod_e :=
          fun x : X => {| equiv_fun := sg_op x; equiv_isequiv := H1 x |}
      |})
  pt =
pt

contents: (** ** Miscellaneous lemmas and corollaries about the Hopf construction *)

goals.conclusion: forall x y : X, transport (hopf_construction X) (merid x) y = x * y
forall x y : X, transport (hopf_construction X) (merid x) y = x * y
transport (hopf_construction X) (merid x) y = x * y
transport idmap (ap (hopf_construction X) (merid x)) y = x * y

contents: refine (ap (fun z => transport idmap z y) _ @ _).

goals.conclusion: transport idmap (path_universe (sg_op x)) y = x * y

contents: (** The connecting map [loops (psusp X) ->* X] associated to the Hopf construction of [X] is a retraction of [loop_susp_unit X] (Proposition 2.19 in https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.02636v1). *)

Proposition hopf_retraction `{Univalence} (X : pType)
  `{IsHSpace X} `{forall a, IsEquiv (a *.)}
  : connecting_map_family (hopf_construction X) o* loop_susp_unit X
    ==* pmap_idmap.

goals.conclusion:
connecting_map_family (hopf_construction X) o* loop_susp_unit X ==*
pmap_idmap

connecting_map_family (hopf_construction X) o* loop_susp_unit X ==*
pmap_idmap
connecting_map_family (hopf_construction X) o* loop_susp_unit X == pmap_idmap
connecting_map_family (hopf_construction X) o* loop_susp_unit X == pmap_idmap

transport (Susp_rec X X (fun x0 : X => path_universe (sg_op x0)))
  (merid x @ (merid pt)^) pt =
x

transport (Susp_rec X X (fun x0 : X => path_universe (sg_op x0))) 
  (merid pt)^
  (transport (Susp_rec X X (fun x0 : X => path_universe (sg_op x0)))
     (merid x) pt) =
x

transport (Susp_rec X X (fun x0 : X => path_universe (sg_op x0))) 
  (merid x) pt =
transport (Susp_rec X X (fun x0 : X => path_universe (sg_op x0))) 
  (merid pt) x

contents: (** It follows from [hopf_retraction] and Freudenthal's theorem that [loop_susp_unit] induces an equivalence on [Pi (2n+1)] for [n]-connected H-spaces (with n >= 0). Note that [X] is automatically left-invertible. *)

(** Since the above equivalence is also a group homomorphism, we get an isomorphism of groups.  (We could also express the conclusion using the wild-category notation [$<~>], but then the goal would involve [HomotopyGroup_type _] instead of literally being in the category [Group].) *)

(** By Freudenthal, [loop_susp_unit] induces an equivalence on lower homotopy groups as well, so it is a (2n+1)-equivalence.  We formalize it below with [m = n-1], and allow [n] to start at [-1].  We prove it using a more general result about reflective subuniverses, [OO_inverts_conn_map_factor_conn_map], but one could also use homotopy groups and the truncated Whitehead theorem. *)

hypotheses.type: loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]
loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]
loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ m).+1) (fun x : X => r (loop_susp_unit X x))

hypotheses.type: loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]
loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]
loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ m).+1) (fun x : X => r (loop_susp_unit X x))

hypotheses.type: loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]

goals.conclusion: IsConnMap (Tr (m +2+ m).+1) (fun x : X => r (loop_susp_unit X x))

hypotheses.type: loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]
loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]
loops (psusp X) ->* [hopf_construction X pt, dpoint (hopf_construction X)]

contents: (** Note that we don't really need the assumption that [X] is left-invertible in the previous result; for [m >= -1], it follows from connectivity.  And for [m = -2], the conclusion is trivial. Here we state the version for [m >= -1] without left-invertibility. *)

(** Here we give a generalization of a result from Eilenberg-Mac Lane Spaces in Homotopy Type Theory, Dan Licata and Eric Finster.  Their version corresponds to [m = -2] in our version.  Their encode-decode proof was formalized in this library in EMSpace.v until this shorter and more general approach was found. *)

goals.conclusion: pTr k X <~>* pTr k (loops (psusp X))

contents: (** Since [loops X] is an H-space, the Hopf construction provides a map [Join (loops X) (loops X) -> Susp (loops X)].  We show that this map is equivalent to the fiber of [loop_susp_counit X : Susp (loops X) -> X] over the base point, up to the automorphism of [Susp (loops X)] induced by inverting loops. *)

Definition pequiv_pfiber_loops_susp_counit_join `{Univalence} (X : pType)
  : pfiber (loop_susp_counit X) <~>* pjoin (loops X) (loops X).

goals.conclusion: forall a : loops X, IsEquiv (sg_op a)
forall a : loops X, IsEquiv (fun y : loops X => y * a)
forall a : loops X, IsEquiv (sg_op a)
forall a : loops X, IsEquiv (fun y : loops X => y * a)

forall a : psusp (loops X),
(fun x : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x = pt) a <~>
hopf_construction (loops X) (?f a)

forall a : psusp (loops X),
(fun x : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x = pt) a <~>
hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) a)

loop_susp_counit X North = pt <~>
hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) North)

loop_susp_counit X South = pt <~>
hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) South)

forall x : loops X,
transport
  (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
   loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
   hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
  (merid x) ?Goal =
?Goal0

forall x : loops X,
transport
  (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
   loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
   hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
  (merid x) 1%equiv =
1%equiv

transport
  (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
   loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
   hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
  (merid p) 1%equiv =
1%equiv

transport
  (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
   loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
   hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
  (merid p) 1%equiv =
1%equiv

  (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
   loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
   hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
  (merid p) 1%equiv q =
1%equiv q

transport
  (fun y : Susp (pt = pt) =>
   Susp_rec pt pt idmap y = pt <~>
   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt) (fun x : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x))
     (functor_susp inverse y))
  (merid p) 1%equiv q =
q

transport
  (fun x : Susp (loops X) =>
   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
     (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0)) 
     (functor_susp inverse x))
  (merid p)
  (1%equiv
     (transport (fun x : Susp (loops X) => Susp_rec pt pt idmap x = pt)
        (merid p)^ q)) =
q

transport
  (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
     (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0)) 
     (functor_susp inverse x))
  (merid p)
  (transport (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) => Susp_rec pt pt idmap x = pt)
     (merid p)^ q) =
q

transport (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) => Susp_rec pt pt idmap x = pt) 
  (merid p)^ q =
?Goal

transport
  (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
     (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0)) 
     (functor_susp inverse x))
  (merid p) ?Goal =
q

transport (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) => Susp_rec pt pt idmap x = pt) 
  (merid p)^ q =
?Goal

transport
  (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
     (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0)) 
     (functor_susp inverse x))
  (merid p) (p @ q) =
q

transport idmap
  (ap
     (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
      Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
        (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0))
        (functor_susp inverse x))
     (merid p))
  (p @ q) =
q

ap
  (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
     (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0)) 
     (functor_susp inverse x))
  (merid p) =
?Goal

ap
  (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
     (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0)) 
     (functor_susp inverse x))
  (merid p) =
?Goal

ap
  (Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt) (fun x : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x)))
  (ap (functor_susp inverse) (merid p)) =
?Goal

ap
  (Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt) (fun x : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x)))
  ?Goal1 =
?Goal

ap
  (Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt) (fun x : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x)))
  (merid p^%path) =
?Goal

equiv_functor_sigma' (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt))
  (Susp_ind
     (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
      (fun x : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x = pt) y <~>
      hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
     (1%equiv
      :
      (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
       (fun x : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x = pt) y <~>
       hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
        North)
     (1%equiv
      :
      (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
       (fun x : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x = pt) y <~>
       hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
        South)
     ((fun p : loops X =>
       path_equiv
         (path_forall
            (transport
               (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
                loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
                hopf_construction (loops X)
                  (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
               (merid p) 1%equiv)
            1%equiv
            ((fun q : loop_susp_counit X South = pt =>
              transport_equiv (merid p) 1 q @
              (ap
                 (transport
                    (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
                     Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
                       (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0))
                       (functor_susp inverse x))
                    (merid p))
                 (transport_paths_Fl (merid p)^ q @
                  whiskerR
                    (ap inverse (ap_V (Susp_rec pt pt idmap) (merid p)) @
                     (inv_V (ap (Susp_rec pt pt idmap) (merid p)) @
                      Susp_rec_beta_merid p))
                    q) @
               (transport_idmap_ap
                  (fun x : Susp (pt = pt) =>
                   Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
                     (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0))
                     (functor_susp inverse x))
                  (merid p) (p @ q) @
                (transport2 idmap
                   (ap_compose (functor_susp inverse)
                      (Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
                         (fun x : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x)))
                      (merid p) @
                    (ap
                       (ap
                          (Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
                             (fun x : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x))))
                       (functor_susp_beta_merid inverse p) @
                     Susp_rec_beta_merid p^))
                   (p @ q) @
                 (transport_path_universe (sg_op p^) (p @ q) @
                  concat_V_pp p q)))
               :
               transport
                 (fun x : Susp (loops X) =>
                  Susp_rec (pt = pt) (pt = pt)
                    (fun x0 : pt = pt => path_universe (sg_op x0))
                    (functor_susp inverse x))
                 (merid p)
                 (1%equiv
                    (transport
                       (fun x : Susp (loops X) => Susp_rec pt pt idmap x = pt)
                       (merid p)^ q)) =
               q)
              :
              transport
                (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
                 loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
                 hopf_construction (loops X)
                   (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
                (merid p) 1%equiv q =
              1%equiv q)
             :
             transport
               (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
                loop_susp_counit X y = pt <~>
                hopf_construction (loops X)
                  (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
               (merid p) 1%equiv ==
             1%equiv)))
      :
      forall x : loops X,
      transport
        (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
         (fun x0 : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x0 = pt) y <~>
         hopf_construction (loops X)
           (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
        (merid x)
        (1%equiv
         :
         (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
          (fun x0 : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x0 = pt) y <~>
          hopf_construction (loops X)
            (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
           North) =
      (1%equiv
       :
       (fun y : Susp (loops X) =>
        (fun x0 : psusp (loops X) => loop_susp_counit X x0 = pt) y <~>
        hopf_construction (loops X) (emap psusp (equiv_path_inverse pt pt) y))
         South)))
  pt =
pt

contents: (** As a corollary we get 2n-connectivity of [loop_susp_counit X] for an n-connected [X]. *)

goals.conclusion:
forall a : X,
IsHProp
  ((fun a0 : X =>
    IsConnected (Tr (n.+1 +2+ n.+1)) (hfiber (loop_susp_counit X) a0)) a)

(fun a : X =>
 IsConnected (Tr (n.+1 +2+ n.+1)) (hfiber (loop_susp_counit X) a)) pt

forall a : X,
IsHProp
  ((fun a0 : X =>
    IsConnected (Tr (n.+1 +2+ n.+1)) (hfiber (loop_susp_counit X) a0)) a)

(fun a : X =>
 IsConnected (Tr (n.+1 +2+ n.+1)) (hfiber (loop_susp_counit X) a)) pt

contents: (** In particular, we get the following result.  All we are really using is that [n.+2 <= n +2+ n], but because of the use of [isconnmap_pred_add], the proof is a bit more specific to this case. *)

Definition pequiv_ptr_psusp_loops `{Univalence} (X : pType) (n : nat) `{IsConnected n.+1 X}
  : pTr n.+2 (psusp (loops X)) <~>* pTr n.+2 X.

goals.conclusion: pTr n.+2 (psusp (loops X)) <~>* pTr n.+2 X
pTr n.+2 (psusp (loops X)) <~>* pTr n.+2 X
pTr n.+2 (psusp (loops X)) ->* pTr n.+2 X
